label: Finger abrasion without tendon or infection red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor finger abrasion.

No repair-needed laceration, deep structure injury, retained foreign body, bite wound, dirty wound requiring separate pathway, infection, diabetic-foot risk when relevant, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Repair-needed laceration, tendon/nerve/blood-vessel injury, joint violation, open fracture, retained foreign body, bite wound, dirty wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, delayed high-risk presentation, diabetic foot when relevant, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports basic cuts and wound-care self-care, keeping wounds clean, clinician-directed tetanus/ointment decisions, and escalation for infection, bleeding, retained foreign body, or deep-structure concern. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed minor abrasions with no deep structure, foreign-body, bite, dirty wound, infection, pregnancy/pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports basic cuts and wound-care self-care, keeping wounds clean, clinician-directed tetanus/ointment decisions, and escalation for infection, bleeding, retained foreign body, or deep-structure concern. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed minor abrasions with no deep structure, foreign-body, bite, dirty wound, infection, pregnancy/pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of finger abrasion
no deep structure injury or retained foreign body
